<file name="/Users/alex/pro/zazencodes-season-3/src/pi-agent-fleet/work/tasks/create-ig-carousel.md">
# Creative Task: Instagram Carousel from Post Draft

## Goal

Turn the attached content (X post draft or research brief) into one publish-ready 3-image Instagram carousel for ZazenCodes.

## Audience

Software engineers learning how to use coding agents, agentic workflows, and modern AI engineering tools.

## Inputs

- The content attached to this run (latest entry in `work/outputs/x-post-draft.md` or `work/outputs/research-ai-engineering.md`).
- ZazenCodes voice: practical agentic coding, first-person, no hype.

## Output Format

- Three square images generated with `generate_image.py`, saved to `../../work/outputs/ig-carousel/` (relative to your working directory) as `slide-1.png`, `slide-2.png`, `slide-3.png`.
- A Markdown summary to stdout with the slide messages, file paths, an Instagram caption, and the next best specialist agent to run.

## Constraints

- Cyberpunk color palette: neon magenta, electric cyan, acid purple on near-black. Visually striking.
- Big, extra-bold uppercase text in every image; 1–5 words per slide, very simple messaging.
- Square images only (the script enforces 1:1 — do not change it).
- Use only facts from the source content. Do not invent stats, quotes, or freshness.
- Caption: no engagement bait or hype words, max one hashtag.

</file>
<file name="/Users/alex/pro/zazencodes-season-3/src/pi-agent-fleet/work/outputs/x-post-draft.md">
# X Post Draft

## Primary post (241 characters)

Coding agents speed up the 16% of your day you spend writing code. They barely touched the other 84%: finding context, switching tools, reconstructing decisions at 3am. METR found experienced devs were 19% slower with AI while believing they were faster.

## Alternate version (226 characters)

Every coding agent is a new hire with no memory. It writes fast but re-asks the same questions, piling context demands on senior engineers. METR found experienced devs were 19% slower with AI while believing they were faster.

## Humanizer audit

- **Draft tell:** Both posts end on the same METR stat. Not a problem for the audience (they see one version), but worth flagging.
- **Rule of three in primary?** "Finding context, switching tools, reconstructing decisions" is three items, but these are the actual categories from the source, not a forced parallel. Kept.
- **No em dashes, no hype words, no hedging, no signposting, no emoji, no passive voice, no AI vocabulary.** Both posts use concrete detail and plain construction.

## Source

https://www.ardel.io/blog/the-3am-problem

## Suggested next agent

`ig-carousel-agent` — the 16%/84% split and the "new hire with no memory" framing both translate well into a 3-slide Instagram carousel with bold text overlays and cyberpunk styling.

</file>
Complete the task in the attached task file using the attached content. Return only the final Markdown output.

**Slide 1:** "AGENTS SPEED UP 16%" — the hook stat
**Slide 2:** "84% IS CONTEXT WORK" — the flip
**Slide 3:** "19% SLOWER. FELT FASTER." — the METR punchline

---

# Instagram Carousel Summary

## Slide messages

| Slide | Text | File |
|-------|------|------|
| 1 | **AGENTS SPEED UP 16%** | `work/outputs/ig-carousel/slide-1.png` |
| 2 | **84% IS CONTEXT WORK** | `work/outputs/ig-carousel/slide-2.png` |
| 3 | **19% SLOWER · FELT FASTER** | `work/outputs/ig-carousel/slide-3.png` |

## Design

Cyberpunk palette (neon magenta, electric cyan, acid purple on near-black). Every slide uses big, extra-bold uppercase text — 3-4 words max. No images of people, no decoration, just the stat hitting hard.

## Instagram caption

Coding agents write fast but re-ask the same questions, piling context debt on senior engineers. METR measured experienced devs 19% slower with AI while reporting they felt faster. #agenticcoding

## Suggested next agent

`comment-reply-agent` — draft replies to likely comments on this carousel (e.g. "but Copilot saves me time", "context window will fix this", "19% is still within noise").
